chunk_summary: Focuses on examination findings and anthropometry in GI disorders and malabsorption to assess nutrition urgency, emphasizing hydration, growth metrics, and systemic signs for triage in India-focused care.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of hydration and intake documentation in managing persistent diarrhea.
Identify anthropometric indicators like Z scores and growth velocity for chronic undernutrition.
Recognize systemic signs such as edema, pallor, and micronutrient deficiencies that escalate nutrition urgency.
Correlate delayed puberty and Tanner staging with disease activity in pediatric IBD.
key_facts: Persistent diarrhea may cause subclinical dehydration; monitor oral intake, urine output, and vomiting.
Falling height velocity or crossing major centile lines indicates chronic undernutrition and mandates escalation.
Edema may signal kwashiorkor or protein-losing enteropathy; muscle wasting and micronutrient signs are critical.
Abdominal distension and visible peristalsis suggest obstruction risk; hepatosplenomegaly may indicate systemic inflammation.
Delayed puberty correlates with poor bone accrual in pediatric IBD; Tanner staging is a disease activity marker.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Children with persistent diarrhea may cycle through subclinical dehydration; record oral intake, urine output, and vomiting so the feeding plan is safe. (WHO Pocket Book of Hospital Care for Children)
document_key_topics: Systemic signs in GI disorders and malabsorption
Hydration and intake documentation in persistent diarrhea
Pubertal staging and growth monitoring in pediatric IBD
